key_hits: Correctly identifies three series (atmospheric CO2 red/Mauna Loa, ocean pCO2 green/ALOHA, ocean pH blue/ALOHA)

key_misses: X-axis end stated as 2020; chart ends at 2018
Claims ocean pCO2 starts at ~320 ppm in 1958 and ocean pH starts ~8.15 in 1958 - both ALOHA series actually begin ~1990, so the 1958 start values are invented

hallucinations: Invented 1958 starting values for the ocean pCO2 (green) and ocean pH (blue) series, which do not exist before ~1990
G4-E4B|t0.1|rep0.justification: Solid structural description of axes/series/stations, but invents pre-1990 values for the two ALOHA series and misreads the X-axis end year (2020 vs 2018).

notes: All nine responses from gemma4-e4b share two consistent errors: (1) the X-axis is read as ending in 2020 when the chart ends in 2018, and (2) the two Station ALOHA series (ocean pCO2 green, ocean pH blue) are given 1958 starting values despite only beginning around 1990 - those early values are invented. Otherwise the model reliably identifies the chart type, all three series with correct colors and stations, both Y-axis ranges (CO2 275-425, pH 8.03-8.33), and the inverse CO2/pH trend. None of the responses mention the inset map of the Hawaiian Islands. The higher-temperature reps (t0.7) introduce additional fabrication: rep1 invents a pH start of ~8.30 and rep2 invents a 'micro-atm' unit for the left axis. Quality is stable at t0.1/t0.4 and degrades at t0.7.
